channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: A Hybrid Key Architecture for Autonomous Agent Credential Management
item.description: AI agents that move money on-chain have a problem nobody talks about cleanly: who holds the keys? That's the problem I ran into building Fishnet, an AI agent transaction security proxy in Rust....

item.title: How I Find & Build Profitable Google Workspace Add-on Ideas
item.description: Hello there This is my first post in this community, so feel free to let me know if something isn’t aligned with the guidelines. Over the last two years, I’ve been building and maintaining Google...

item.title: The uncomfortable truth about European cloud sovereignty
item.description: My recent blog post about AWS European Sovereign Cloud generated more backlash than I anticipated. The core criticism was simple and sharp: AWS is still a US company. No matter how many legal...

item.title: Cognitive Property: Who Owns the Way You Think?
item.description: AI tools picking up and repeating your habits isn't new. ChatGPT does it by design — it mirrors your tone, adapts to your preferences, and learns what you respond well to. The phenomenon has...

item.title: Revamped RSS Feed Imports
item.description: You can now add multiple RSS feed imports, monitor their status, and assign each one to different default authors or organizations. DEV (and Forem) have supported RSS feed imports for a long time....

item.title: SQL Injection – UNION attack, retrieving data from other tables | PortSwigger Lab Note #4
item.description: target： Lab URL:...

item.title: State of AI Assets Q1 2026 — 143K agents, 17K MCP servers, all trust scored
item.description: State of AI Assets — Q1 2026 The first open census of the AI agent ecosystem. 5M+ assets. 143K agents & tools. All trust scored. We built Nerq to answer a simple question: what's actually out...

item.title: I got tired of color contrast tools that break brand colors. So I built my own.
item.description: You run a contrast check. It fails. You change the color. It passes WCAG. But now it looks nothing like your brand. Every designer knows this moment. And most contrast tools just leave you there....

item.title: The Context Loading Order: The Hidden Variable That Controls Your AI Agent's Behavior
item.description: Most AI agent builders focus on what's in the context. The order it loads in matters just as much. Why Loading Order Is Your Agent's Value Hierarchy When your agent boots, it builds a picture of...

item.title: 5 Tools That Changed How I Give Live Coding Presentations on Mac
item.description: Recording coding tutorials and giving live demos for almost 10 years taught me one thing: the tools you use to present code matter just as much as the code itself. We've all been there. You're...

item.title: Feeld Was a Dating App for the Freaks. Now Some People Call It ‘Normie Hell’
item.description: The app that catered to unconventional kinks is gaining steam among daters with vanilla preferences—and some aren’t happy about it.

item.title: Panic’s gaming ambitions hinge on the weird and whimsical
item.description: A game about an annoying goose with a button dedicated entirely to honking isn't the obvious recipe for a hit. But doing the less-obvious thing has become the norm for Panic's gaming efforts,...

item.title: The iPhone 17E is good, but you probably shouldn’t buy it
item.description: The iPhone 17E is a better value than the 16E was when it arrived, but that should matter to basically nobody. The main thing to know about the 17E is that the iPhone 17 exists, it costs $200 more...

item.title: Apple iPad Air M4 review: a little bit faster now
item.description: The newest iPad Air is a chip bump iPad - maybe the chip-bump-iest iPad Air yet. Inside this new machine are, in fact, three upgraded chips compared to last year's model: an M4 processor, a C1X...

item.title: How Live Nation allegedly terrorized the concert industry
item.description: SeatGeek was close to a deal that would bring its ticketing business to the next level. The company was in negotiations with the Dallas Cowboys, aiming to take over first-party sales at its...

item.title: Can the Security Platform Finally Deliver for the Mid-Market?
item.description: Mid-market organizations are constantly striving to achieve security levels on a par with their enterprise peers. With heightened awareness of supply chain attacks, your customers and business...

item.title: New Attack Against Wi-Fi
item.description: It’s called AirSnitch: Unlike previous Wi-Fi attacks, AirSnitch exploits core features in Layers 1 and 2 and the failure to bind and synchronize a client across these and higher layers, other...

item.title: Chrome Extension Turns Malicious After Ownership Transfer, Enabling Code Injection and Data Theft
item.description: Two Google Chrome extensions have turned malicious after what appears to be a case of ownership transfer, offering attackers a way to push malware to downstream customers, inject arbitrary code,...

item.title: Can AI Kill the Venture Capitalist?
item.description: VCs are betting that artificial intelligence will disrupt nearly every industry in the world. Are they prepared for it to disrupt their own?

item.title: Web Server Exploits and Mimikatz Used in Attacks Targeting Asian Critical Infrastructure
item.description: High-value organizations located in South, Southeast, and East Asia have been targeted by a Chinese threat actor as part of a years-long campaign. The activity, which has targeted aviation,...

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with...

item.title: Will the Pentagon’s Anthropic controversy scare startups away from defense work?
item.description: On the latest episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, we discussed what the controversy means for other startups seeking to work with the federal government.

item.title: Apple is going high-end with new ‘Ultra’ products next
item.description: Fresh off launching the low-cost MacBook Neo, Apple is reportedly preparing at least three new products that will fit into its highest-end "ultra" lineup. According to Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, the...

item.title: Owner of ICE detention facility sees big opportunity in AI man camps
item.description: AI data center developers are increasingly relying on a style of camp popularized as housing for men working in remote oil fields.

item.title: Listen to this: Mabe Fratti’s experimental cello pop
item.description: The opening notes of "Kravitz," which kicks off Mabe Fratti's 2024 record Sentir Que No Sabes, are lodged in my brain permanently. It's not a showy album, by any means. But there's something about...

item.title: What we’re listening to, watching, and reading right now.
item.description: There is so much art out there that it’s absolutely impossible to keep up. Whether it’s a slept-on post-punk album from the ’80s, a new sci-fi novel, or a cult classic horror movie, we’re always...

item.title: The best Switch 2 controller just got better (and a little worse)
item.description: Crowning the EasySMX S10 the best Switch 2 controller last fall was easy. The $60 wireless gamepad, which is often well below $50, mimics or improves on many of the $90 Switch 2 Pro controller's...

item.title: The cute and cursed story of Furby
item.description: The hottest toy of 1998 was sort of adorable, and sort of annoying. It couldn't do much - couldn't do anything, really - but it could look at you, it could say some nonsense phrases, and it seemed...

item.title: The uncomfortable truth about hybrid vehicles
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on the EV struggle, follow Andrew J. Hawkins. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers'...

item.title: A roadmap for AI, if anyone will listen
item.description: The Pro-Human Declaration was finalized before last week's Pentagon-Anthropic standoff, but the collision of the two events wasn’t lost on anyone involved.
